name: dotdotpwn
title: dotdotpwn

commands.name: dotdotpwn

arguments.description: Module [http | http-url | ftp | tftp | payload | stdout]
arguments.raw: -m	Module [http | http-url | ftp | tftp | payload | stdout]
-h	Hostname

arguments.description: Operating System detection for intelligent fuzzing (nmap)
arguments.raw: -O	Operating System detection for intelligent fuzzing (nmap)

arguments.description: Operating System type if known ("windows", "unix" or "generic")
arguments.raw: -o	Operating System type if known ("windows", "unix" or "generic")
-s	Service version detection (banner grabber)

arguments.metavar: Depth of traversals e.g. deepness 3 equals to ../../../; default
arguments.raw: -d	Depth of traversals (e.g. deepness 3 equals to ../../../; default: 6)

arguments.description: according to OS detected, defaults in TraversalEngine.pm)
arguments.raw: -f	Specific filename (e.g. /etc/motd; default: according to OS detected, defaults in TraversalEngine.pm)

arguments.description: Add @Extra_files in TraversalEngine.pm (e.g. web.config, httpd.conf, etc.)
arguments.raw: -E	Add @Extra_files in TraversalEngine.pm (e.g. web.config, httpd.conf, etc.)

arguments.metavar: Use SSL for HTTP and Payload module not needed for http-url use a https
arguments.raw: -S	Use SSL for HTTP and Payload module (not needed for http-url, use a https:// url instead)

arguments.metavar: URL with the part to be fuzzed marked as TRAVERSAL e.g. http
arguments.raw: -u	URL with the part to be fuzzed marked as TRAVERSAL (e.g. http://foo:8080/id.php?x=TRAVERSAL&y=31337)

arguments.metavar: Text pattern to match in the response http-url & payload modules - e.g. "root
arguments.raw: -k	Text pattern to match in the response (http-url & payload modules - e.g. "root:" if trying /etc/passwd)

arguments.description: Filename with the payload to be sent and the part to be fuzzed marked with the TRAVERSAL keyword
arguments.raw: -p	Filename with the payload to be sent and the part to be fuzzed marked with the TRAVERSAL keyword
-x	Port to connect (default: HTTP=80; FTP=21; TFTP=69)

arguments.metavar: Time in milliseconds between each test default
arguments.raw: -t	Time in milliseconds between each test (default: 300 (.3 second))

arguments.description: Use the Bisection Algorithm to detect the exact deepness once a vulnerability has been found
arguments.raw: -X	Use the Bisection Algorithm to detect the exact deepness once a vulnerability has been found

arguments.description: File extension appended at the end of each fuzz string (e.g. ".php", ".jpg", ".inc")
arguments.raw: -e	File extension appended at the end of each fuzz string (e.g. ".php", ".jpg", ".inc")
-U	Username (default: 'anonymous')
-P	Password (default: 'dot@dot.pwn')

arguments.metavar: HTTP Method to use when using the 'http' module GET POST HEAD COPY MOVE default
arguments.raw: -M	HTTP Method to use when using the 'http' module [GET | POST | HEAD | COPY | MOVE] (default: GET)
-r	Report filename (default: 'HOST_MM-DD-YYYY_HOUR-MIN.txt')

arguments.description: Break after the first vulnerability is found
arguments.raw: -b	Break after the first vulnerability is found
-q	Quiet mode (doesn't print each attempt)

arguments.description: Continue if no data was received from host
arguments.raw: -C	Continue if no data was received from host

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# dotdotpwn -h
 #################################################################################
 #                                                                               #
 #  CubilFelino                                                       Chatsubo   #
 #  Security Research Lab              and            [(in)Security Dark] Labs   #
 #  chr1x.sectester.net                             chatsubo-labs.blogspot.com   #
 #                                                                               #
 #                               pr0udly present:                                #
 #                                                                               #
 #  ________            __  ________            __  __________                   #
 #  \______ \    ____ _/  |_\______ \    ____ _/  |_\______   \__  _  __ ____    #
 #   |    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|     ___/\ \/ \/ //    \   #
 #   |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    |     \     /|   |  \  #
 #  /_______  / \____/ |__| /_______  / \____/ |__|  |____|      \/\_/ |___|  /  #
 #          \/                      \/                                      \/   #
 #                              - DotDotPwn v3.0.2 -                             #
 #                         The Directory Traversal Fuzzer                        #
 #                         http://dotdotpwn.sectester.net                        #
 #                            dotdotpwn@sectester.net                            #
 #                                                                               #
 #                               by chr1x & nitr0us                              #
 #################################################################################
 
 Usage: ./dotdotpwn.pl -m <module> -h <host> [OPTIONS]
 	Available options:
 	-m	Module [http | http-url | ftp | tftp | payload | stdout]
 	-h	Hostname
 	-O	Operating System detection for intelligent fuzzing (nmap)
 	-o	Operating System type if known ("windows", "unix" or "generic")
 	-s	Service version detection (banner grabber)
 	-d	Depth of traversals (e.g. deepness 3 equals to ../../../; default: 6)
 	-f	Specific filename (e.g. /etc/motd; default: according to OS detected, defaults in TraversalEngine.pm)
 	-E	Add @Extra_files in TraversalEngine.pm (e.g. web.config, httpd.conf, etc.)
 	-S	Use SSL for HTTP and Payload module (not needed for http-url, use a https:// url instead)
 	-u	URL with the part to be fuzzed marked as TRAVERSAL (e.g. http://foo:8080/id.php?x=TRAVERSAL&y=31337)
 	-k	Text pattern to match in the response (http-url & payload modules - e.g. "root:" if trying /etc/passwd)
 	-p	Filename with the payload to be sent and the part to be fuzzed marked with the TRAVERSAL keyword
 	-x	Port to connect (default: HTTP=80; FTP=21; TFTP=69)
 	-t	Time in milliseconds between each test (default: 300 (.3 second))
 	-X	Use the Bisection Algorithm to detect the exact deepness once a vulnerability has been found
 	-e	File extension appended at the end of each fuzz string (e.g. ".php", ".jpg", ".inc")
 	-U	Username (default: 'anonymous')
 	-P	Password (default: 'dot@dot.pwn')
 	-M	HTTP Method to use when using the 'http' module [GET | POST | HEAD | COPY | MOVE] (default: GET)
 	-r	Report filename (default: 'HOST_MM-DD-YYYY_HOUR-MIN.txt')
 	-b	Break after the first vulnerability is found
 	-q	Quiet mode (doesn't print each attempt)
 	-C	Continue if no data was received from host

examples.command:
dotdotpwn.pl -m http -h 192.168.1.1 -M GET
#################################################################################
#                                                                               #
#  CubilFelino                                                       Chatsubo   #
#  Security Research Lab              and            [(in)Security Dark] Labs   #
#  chr1x.sectester.net                             chatsubo-labs.blogspot.com   #
#                                                                               #
#                               pr0udly present:                                #
#                                                                               #
#  ________            __  ________            __  __________                   #
#  \______ \    ____ _/  |_\______ \    ____ _/  |_\______   \__  _  __ ____    #
#   |    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|     ___/\ \/ \/ //    \   #
#   |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    |     \     /|   |  \  #
#  /_______  / \____/ |__| /_______  / \____/ |__|  |____|      \/\_/ |___|  /  #
#          \/                      \/                                      \/   #
#                               - DotDotPwn v3.0 -                              #
#                         The Directory Traversal Fuzzer                        #
#                         http://dotdotpwn.sectester.net                        #
#                            dotdotpwn@sectester.net                            #
#                                                                               #
#                               by chr1x & nitr0us                              #
#################################################################################

[+] Report name: Reports/192.168.1.1_05-20-2014_08-41.txt

[========== TARGET INFORMATION ==========]
[+] Hostname: 192.168.1.1
[+] Protocol: http
[+] Port: 80

[=========== TRAVERSAL ENGINE ===========]
[+] Creating Traversal patterns (mix of dots and slashes)
[+] Multiplying 6 times the traversal patterns (-d switch)
[+] Creating the Special Traversal patterns
[+] Translating (back)slashes in the filenames
[+] Adapting the filenames according to the OS type detected (generic)
[+] Including Special sufixes
[+] Traversal Engine DONE ! - Total traversal tests created: 19680

[=========== TESTING RESULTS ============]
[+] Ready to launch 3.33 traversals per second
[+] Press Enter to start the testing (You can stop it pressing Ctrl + C)

#################################################################################
#                                                                               #
#  CubilFelino                                                       Chatsubo   #
#  Security Research Lab              and            [(in)Security Dark] Labs   #
#  chr1x.sectester.net                             chatsubo-labs.blogspot.com   #

#  \______ \    ____ _/  |_\______ \    ____ _/  |_\______   \__  _  __ ____    #
#   |    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|    |  \  /  _ \\   __\|     ___/\ \/ \/ //    \   #
#   |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    `   \(  <_> )|  |  |    |     \     /|   |  \  #
#  /_______  / \____/ |__| /_______  / \____/ |__|  |____|      \/\_/ |___|  /  #
#          \/                      \/                                      \/   #
#                              - DotDotPwn v3.0.2 -                             #
#                         The Directory Traversal Fuzzer                        #
#                         http://dotdotpwn.sectester.net                        #
#                            dotdotpwn@sectester.net                            #
#                                                                               #
#                               by chr1x & nitr0us                              #
#################################################################################

Usage: ./dotdotpwn.pl -m <module> -h <host> [OPTIONS]
	Available options:
	-m	Module [http | http-url | ftp | tftp | payload | stdout]
	-h	Hostname
	-O	Operating System detection for intelligent fuzzing (nmap)
	-o	Operating System type if known ("windows", "unix" or "generic")
	-s	Service version detection (banner grabber)
	-d	Depth of traversals (e.g. deepness 3 equals to ../../../; default: 6)
	-f	Specific filename (e.g. /etc/motd; default: according to OS detected, defaults in TraversalEngine.pm)
	-E	Add @Extra_files in TraversalEngine.pm (e.g. web.config, httpd.conf, etc.)
	-S	Use SSL for HTTP and Payload module (not needed for http-url, use a https:// url instead)
	-u	URL with the part to be fuzzed marked as TRAVERSAL (e.g. http://foo:8080/id.php?x=TRAVERSAL&y=31337)
	-k	Text pattern to match in the response (http-url & payload modules - e.g. "root:" if trying /etc/passwd)
	-p	Filename with the payload to be sent and the part to be fuzzed marked with the TRAVERSAL keyword
	-x	Port to connect (default: HTTP=80; FTP=21; TFTP=69)
	-t	Time in milliseconds between each test (default: 300 (.3 second))
	-X	Use the Bisection Algorithm to detect the exact deepness once a vulnerability has been found
	-e	File extension appended at the end of each fuzz string (e.g. ".php", ".jpg", ".inc")
	-U	Username (default: 'anonymous')
	-P	Password (default: 'dot@dot.pwn')
	-M	HTTP Method to use when using the 'http' module [GET | POST | HEAD | COPY | MOVE] (default: GET)
	-r	Report filename (default: 'HOST_MM-DD-YYYY_HOUR-MIN.txt')
	-b	Break after the first vulnerability is found
	-q	Quiet mode (doesn't print each attempt)
	-C	Continue if no data was received from host

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Directory Traversal Fuzzer.
DotDotPwn is a very flexible intelligent fuzzer to discover traversal
directory vulnerabilities in software such as HTTP/FTP/TFTP
servers, Web platforms such as CMSs, ERPs, Blogs, etc.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
